ON OCT-20-2009 IN DEPT. 206, PLAINTIFFS RONALD AND CAROL PALMER'S MOTION TO SPECIALLY SET A PREFERENTIAL TRIAL DATE GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A). THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 2/8/2010 @ 11:15AM IN DEPT. 206. COUNSEL TO CONTACT DEPT. 502 FOR SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE DATES. ALL DISCOVERY MOTIONS AND OTHER MOTIONS AFFECTING THE TRIAL DATE TO BE HEARD IN DEPT. 206. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL THE CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL COURT. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULATED TO. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL IDENTIFICATION AND LAY WITNESSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS THAT SUPPORT THEIR TESTIMONY, AS TO EACH INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT BY 11/19/09. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES AFTER THE DUE DATE THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT IDENTIFIED PREVIOUSLY. ALL DEFENDANTS SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL LAY WITNESSES THAT THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT LEARNED OF THROUGH ITS OWN DISCOVERY EFFORTS AND THAT WILL SUPPORT PROPOSITION 51 PURPOSES OR FOR THEIR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS UPON WHICH THESE WITNESSES SUPPORT, AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 12/9/09. IF DEFENDANTS LEARN OF NEW WITNESSES AND OR DOCUMENTS, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED PREVIOUSLY. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. WRITTEN ORDER PENDING. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; CLERK: E. BURA; REPORTER: MARY ELLEN SCHERER CSR# 5208 (206)

TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED - DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO PRESENT 'NEW OR DIFFERENT FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCE, OR LAW' THAT WARRANT RECONSIDERATION. CCP 1008(A). THE DENIAL OF DEFENDANT'S PRIOR MOTION IS NOT INCONSISTENT WITH THE GRANTING OF DANA COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. IN DANA'S MOTION THE PLAINTIFF LACKED DEFENDANT-SPECIFIC EVIDENCE SUFFICIENT TO WARRANT A TRIAL ON THE ISSUE OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES. BY CONTRAST, IN PNEUMO ABEX'S MOTION PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES CONTAINED SEVERAL FACTS SPECIFIC TO THE DEFENDANT AND BARRY CASTLEMAN HAD A FOUNDATION UPON WHICH TO BASE HIS OPINION THAT DEFENDANT WAS AWARE OF THE HAZARDS OF ASBESTOS. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: CANDACE YOUNT CSR # 2737

Defendant Pneumo Abex Llc Successor In Interest To Abex Defendant Pneumo Abex Llc Successor In Interest To Abex Corporation Erroneously Sued Herein As Pneumo-Abexs' Motion For Reconsideration (Of Its Order Dated January 28,2010)
Off Calendar Mar-24-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Shelley K. Tinkoff, Esq. of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / moving party Pneumo Abex Llc Successor In Interest To Abex Defendant Pneumo Abex Llc Successor In Interest To Abex Corporation Erroneously Sued Herein As Pneumo-Abexs
No other appearances.

RULING:
Defendant Pneumo Abex Llc Successor In Interest To Abex Defendant Pneumo Abex Llc Successor In Interest To Abex Corporation Erroneously Sued Herein As Pneumo-Abexs' motion for reconsideration of the denial of its motion for summary adjudication on January 28, 2010. Defendant submitted to tentative ruling. No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted. Defendant's motion is denied. Defendant failed to present 'new or different facts, circumstance, or law' that warrant reconsideration. CCP 1008(a). The denial of defendant's prior motion is not inconsistent with the granting of Dana Company's motion for summary adjudication. In Dana's motion the plaintiff lacked defendant-specific evidence sufficient to warrant a trial on the issue of punitive damages. By contrast, in Pneumo Abex's motion plaintiff's responses to special interrogatories contained several facts specific to the defendant and Barry Castleman had a foundation upon which to base his opinion that defendant was aware of the hazards of asbestos. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Candace Yount CSR # 2737 =(220/HEK)

Defendant Pneumo Abex Llc'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Thomas J. Moses, Esq. of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / moving party Pneumo Abex LLC
Stephen Tigerman, Esq. of HAROWITZ & TIGERMAN (415-788-1588) for plaintiff / opposing party Ronald Palmer

RULING:
Defendant Pneumo-Abex's motion for summary adjudication as to punitive damages. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production since plaintiff's interrogatory responses were not factually devoid. Further, Dr. Castleman's declaration and Pneumo-Abex' s interrogatory responses create a triable issue whether Pneumo-Abex knew its asbestos-containing products were harmful at a time before plaintiff claims exposure to them yet defendant continued to market them. Pneumo-Abex's objections to Dr Castleman's declaration are overruled. Dr. Castleman has sufficient foundation and expertise to base his statements that Abex knew of the dangers of asbestos. The Court would not consider the supplemental declaration of Dr. Castleman. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Defendant Carlisle Corp.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-27-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jeremy D. Huie, Esq. of BASSI EDLIN HUIE & BLUM (415-403-4429), appearing for defendant / moving party Carlisle Corporation
Stephen Tigerman, Esq. of HAROWITZ & TIGERMAN (415-788-1588) for plaintiff / opposing party Roland Palmer

RULING:
Defendant Carlisle Corporation's motion for summary judgment. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is denied. Defendant fails to sustain its burden of production. Defendant overstates Dicola's holding and applicability to the instant action. Mr. Palmer is testifying to his personal knowledge as it relates to his work with Carlisle brake blocks from 1962 to 1969. Unlike the plaintiff in Dicola, Mr. Palmer worked with the defendant's brake blocks "regularly" and his description of the packaging in question provides circumstantial evidence of its manufacturer and/or distributor. The Court overruled the hearsay objections. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077
